summary: Music program featuring Beethoven's Cello Sonatas Op. 5, No. 1 and No. 2, and Violin Sonata Op. 12, No. 1. The program explores early Beethoven works where he transitions from classical norms to a more declarative style, including the first cello sonatas with a fully written piano part.

A vocal and instrumental program featuring works by women composers, including Clara Schumann's 'Ich stand in dunkeln Träumen' and Pauline Viardot's 'Madrid.' Performers include Miriam Blatt, Clara Blazer, and others, with a focus on chamber works by women composers.

W.F. Bach Pastorale in A minor, Boismortier Sonatas Op. 37, Platti Trio in C minor, and Chopin Cello Sonata in G minor Op. 65. The program notes discuss the sentimental Siciliano style and the history of Boismortier's trios.

Works by Hélène Liebmann (Grand Trio in A major Op. 11), Miriam Hyde (Trio for Flute, Clarinet, and Piano), and Elfrida Andrée (Piano Quartet). The program highlights female composers from the 19th and 20th centuries.

Features Mozart's Flute Concerto No. 1, Telemann's Trio Sonata TWV 42:c2, and C.P.E. Bach's Organ Sonata Wq 70/6 and Trio Sonata Wq 146. The program emphasizes Baroque and early Classical chamber music for flute, violin, and organ. Program includes Offenbach Cello Duets Op. 53 No. 1, Mozart Flute Concerto in G major KV 313, Quantz Trio Sonata in C minor, and Telemann Trio Sonata TWV 42:e2. It features virtuosic cello and flute works from the Classical and Baroque periods.

Arrangements for piano trio including Beethoven's Symphony No. 5 (arranged by Liszt and Choe), Mozart's Piano Trio K. 548, Mendelssohn's Piano Trio No. 2 Op. 66, and Schubert's Rondo Op. 107 for piano four-hands.

Baroque trio sonatas by Quantz (QV 2:Anh.5) and Telemann (TWV 42:e2), followed by Myaskovsky's Cello Sonata No. 1 and Joe Hisaishi's pieces from Studio Ghibli animations. Similar to Entry 8, featuring Quantz and Telemann trio sonatas, Myaskovsky's Cello Sonata No. 1, Hisaishi's Studio Ghibli pieces, and Prokofiev's Overture on Hebrew Themes.

Program featuring Vivaldi's Concerto for Two Cellos RV531, Edvard Grieg's Violin Sonata No. 2 Op. 13, and Mendelssohn's Piano Trio No. 2 Op. 66. Includes historical notes on Vivaldi's work at the Ospedale della Pieta in Venice.
Telemann's Nouveaux Quartuors TWV 43:D3, Mozart's Divertimento K. 136, traditional Chinese folk music, and Grieg's Violin Sonata No. 2 Op. 13.

Program celebrating women composers including Emilie Mayer's Quartet in G minor Op. 14, Mélanie Bonis's Suite, and Amanda Röntgen-Maier's Piano Trio in Eb. Mayer was often called the 'female Beethoven' for her significant 19th-century works.
Short pieces for violin, cello, and piano by Elgar, Fauré, and Beethoven, followed by Haydn's String Quartet Op. 76 No. 3, Mozart's Violin Sonata K301, and traditional Chinese pieces.

Dvořák's Piano Trio No. 2 Op. 26 (first three movements), Beethoven's Violin Sonata No. 5 'Spring', Schumann's Arabesque Op. 18, and Beethoven's Piano Sonata No. 30 Op. 109. Vivaldi's Sonata No. 1 for Alto Flute, Dvořák's Piano Trio No. 2 Op. 26 (middle movements), Mignone's Waltzes for Solo Bassoon, and Brahms's Violin Sonata No. 1 Op. 78.

Vivaldi's Concerto for Two Violins RV522, Cassadó's Cello Sonata in A minor, Ravel's Pièce en Forme de Habanera, Chopin's Cello Sonata Op. 65, and several Chinese and Australian string quartet arrangements.

A Christmas-themed program featuring choral works by Lasso, Praetorius, Bruch, and others, alongside Gretchaninov's In aller Frühe Op. 126b, Ravel's Habanera, Telemann's Quartet in D minor, and Vivaldi's Concerto for Two Violins RV 565.

A concert of music by women composers, including Clara Schumann's Romances for Violin and Piano Op. 22, Louise Farrenc's Trio for Flute, Cello, and Piano Op. 45, and Clara Schumann's Piano Trio Op. 17 and songs.
A program featuring clarinet works by Grundman, Gounod, and others, alongside Grieg's Violin Sonata No. 3 Op. 45 and Robert Schumann's Piano Quintet in E Flat Op. 44.

Program featuring Beethoven's '7 Variations on Bei Männern, welche Liebe fühlen', Brahms's Violin Sonata No. 1 Op. 78, and Poulenc's Trio for piano, oboe, and bassoon. Notes discuss Beethoven's admiration for Mozart and Poulenc's unique 20th-century style which blends lightness with emotional depth.

Features Robert Schumann's Piano Quintet Op. 44, Richard Kershaw's 'Elephant Dances' for bassoon and piano, and Rachmaninoff's Cello Sonata Op. 19. The program highlights Schumann's revolutionary quintet and Rachmaninoff's melodic cello writing from his post-1901 period.

Joseph Haydn's Piano Trio No. 39 'Gypsy', a selection of vocal duets by Vivaldi, Fanny Mendelssohn, Purcell, and Mozart, and Felix Mendelssohn's Piano Trio No. 2 Op. 66. The notes focus on the Hungarian 'Gypsy' style of Haydn's famous trio.

Tango-themed program featuring Piazzolla's tangos (Primavera Portena, Oblivion, Libertango) and Jacob Gade's 'Jealousy', followed by Saint-Saëns's Violin Sonata No. 1 and a selection of Christmas music by Arbeau, Praetorius, Morales, and others.

Beethoven's 'Kreutzer' Sonata Op. 47 for violin and piano, Georg Philipp Telemann's Concerto for Two Violas, and Beethoven's String Quartet Op. 18 No. 1. The notes describe the 'outrageous' and tempestuous nature of the Kreutzer sonata.

Brahms's Violin Sonata No. 3 Op. 108, Mozart's 'Parto, Parto' from La Clemenza di Tito, Terence Greaves's 'A Garden of Weeds', and Poulenc's Trio for flute, bassoon, and piano. The Brahms sonata is noted for its endearing Adagio.

Telemann's Quartet TWV 43:A1 (arranged for wind quintet and guitar), Fauré's Piano Quartet Op. 15, and Harris's Divertimento for wind quintet and piano. The Telemann work is characterized as a set of dances for an 18th-century party.
Kristofer Spike's 'West Highland Wander' for string quartet, Vivaldi's 'Winter' from The Four Seasons, and a selection of Christmas choral music by Boris Ord, Kathryn Rose, Palestrina, and Marenzio.

Shostakovich's 'Five Pieces for Two Violins and Piano', Borodin's String Quartet No. 2 in D major, and Brahms's Piano Quintet Op. 34. Borodin's quartet is described as a 20th wedding anniversary gift for his wife.

Beethoven's Violin Sonata No. 2 Op. 12, Henri Dutilleux's Sonatina for Flute and Piano (1943), and Handel's Sonata in G minor. The notes discuss the early success of Beethoven in Vienna and Dutilleux's meticulous composition style.

Charles Koechlin's Divertissement Op. 90, Paul Schoenfield's 'Café Music', and Schubert's Trio No. 1 in B flat Major. Koechlin's work reflects his late Impressionistic period and interest in diverse musical styles. Similar to Entry 30, featuring Charles Koechlin's Divertissement Op. 90, Paul Schoenfield's 'Café Music', and Schubert's Trio No. 1 in B flat Major. The program explores a range of styles from Impressionism to Jazz-influenced chamber music.

Brahms program including 'Regenlied' Op. 59 and the Violin Sonata in G major Op. 78, alongside a selection of songs from musicals and operas by Nicolai, Massenet, Puccini, and Loewe. Song texts and synopses for vocal works by Nicolai (Merry Wives of Windsor), Puccini (La Bohème), Massenet (Manon), and Loewe (My Fair Lady).

C.P.E. Bach's Trio Sonata in B minor, Josef Suk's Piano Quartet in A minor, Arvo Pärt's 'Spiegel im Spiegel', and Mozart's String Duo No. 1 in G major. Pärt's work is described as a literal musical representation of mirrors reflecting each other.

Mozart's Piano Quartet K478, Mendelssohn's Piano Trio in C minor, Dvořák's Piano Trio in F minor, songs by Tchaikovsky and Vaughan Williams, Kodály's Duo for violin and cello, and Bartók's Romanian Dances. Song texts and translations for Tchaikovsky's 'A Pleading' and Vaughan Williams's 'Silent Noon' from the cycle 'The House of Life'.

Debussy's 'Eight Selected Pieces' arranged for flute and piano, Fazil Say's 'The Ballad of Gezi Park', Beethoven's Violin Sonata No. 8, and Fauré's Piano Quartet No. 1. Say's work is a wordless meditation on political events in Turkey.

Mozart's Violin Sonata K 454, a selection of Romantic/Nationalist songs by Chausson, Grieg, and Rimsky-Korsakov, Baroque songs by Caccini and Scarlatti, Grieg's Violin Sonata No. 1, and Schubert's 'The Shepherd on the Rock'.

Vocal program of Mozart arias and duets, Chris Palmer's 'Fantasia Brilliante' on Verdi's Un Ballo in Maschera for bassoon, Heitor Villa-Lobos's Suite for Voice and Violin, and Brahms's Viola Sonata Op. 120 No. 1.

A program of Medieval and Renaissance music featuring I Cantarini singing madrigals by Palestrina, Pederson, and Verdelot. It also includes music for recorders by Holborne and music for viols by Susato, Byrd, and Ortiz, illustrating the transition from Renaissance to Baroque styles.

Features Köhler's Concert Duet Variations on a Schubert melody, Brahms's Piano Quintet in F minor (Scherzo and Finale), Debussy's Syrinx for solo flute, Miriam Hyde's piano works, Piazzolla's Le Grand Tango, and Mahler's songs arranged for wind quintet.

Program focusing on John Jenkins (1592-1678) and other English fantasy composers for viols, as well as works by Clive Lane and John Coprario. It explores the 'instrumental madrigal' form and the influence of Italian musicians on the English court during the 16th and 17th centuries. Renaissance and Baroque program featuring music for viols by John Jenkins and Richard Mico, recorder consort music by Anthony Holborne, and vocal works by Giaches de Wert and Claudio Monteverdi. The program explores the development of the instrumental 'fancy' or 'fantasy'.

Features César Franck's Sonata in A (arranged for cello), Gabriel Fauré's five songs including 'Après un rêve', and Darius Milhaud's Suite for piano, violin, and clarinet. The Franck sonata is noted for its cyclic structure where themes reappear transformed across movements. Features César Franck's Sonata in A, Gabriel Fauré's songs, and Darius Milhaud's Suite Op. 157b. Milhaud's work is described as having a leaner and cooler aesthetic, rejecting the sumptuous orchestral textures of the previous era.

Duplicate of Entry 46, featuring Grieg's Violin Sonata No. 1, Fauré's songs, and Elgar's String Quartet Op. 83. Includes detailed notes on the Norwegian folk influences (hardingfele) in Grieg's work. Grieg's Violin Sonata No. 1 in F major Op. 8, Fauré's five songs, and Elgar's String Quartet Op. 83. The notes discuss Grieg's early career and the spring-like freshness of his Op. 8, which overwhelming sonata-form with lyrical ideas. Song texts and translations for Fauré's five songs, including 'Mandoline', 'Nell', and 'Les berceaux'. The texts explore themes of love, nature, and the sea through the poetry of Verlaine, de Lisle, and Prudhomme.

'Songs of Love and Death' program featuring music for viols by Binchois and others, Händel and Mozart arias for soprano and string quartet, and Schubert's 'Death and the Maiden' quartet. The program spans from the 15th to the 19th centuries. Duplicate of Entry 49, 'Songs of Love and Death' program featuring a range of vocal and instrumental works from the 15th to the 20th centuries, including songs by Schumann, Schubert, and Puccini.

Brahms's Violin Sonata No. 1 Op. 78 (first movement), Vivaldi's Concerto for 2 Guitars, Albinoni's Adagio, and Shostakovich's Five Pieces for 2 Violins and Piano. The notes highlight the 'Rain Sonata' nickname for Brahms's Op. 78. Duplicate of Entry 51, featuring works by Vivaldi, Albinoni, Brahms, Shostakovich, and Mendelssohn's Piano Trio No. 2 Op. 66.

Duplicate of Entry 53, featuring J.S. Bach's Cantata BWV 209, C.P.E. Bach's Quartet in A minor, and Beethoven's String Quartet Op. 18 No. 4. J.S. Bach's Cantata BWV 209, C.P.E. Bach's Quartet in A minor Wq 93, and Beethoven's String Quartet Op. 18 No. 4. The notes explore the mystery of the authorship of BWV 209 and the 'sensitive style' (empfindsamer Stil) of C.P.E. Bach.

Duplicate of Entry 55, featuring Glazunov's 'Quartet Slav', Boccherini's Cello Sonata, and Schumann's Piano Quartet Op. 47. Glazunov's 'Quartet Slav' Op. 26, Boccherini's Cello Sonata in C major, and Robert Schumann's Piano Quartet in E flat major Op. 47. Glazunov's quartet is described as a work based entirely on Slavic melodies.

Beethoven's String Trio Op. 9 No. 1, Grieg's Cello Sonata Op. 36, vocal pieces by Schubert, Kalliwoda, and Walker, and Beethoven's Piano Quartet Op. 16(a). Notes highlight Grieg's passionate and Norwegian-influenced cello sonata. Duplicate of Entry 57, featuring Beethoven's String Trio, Grieg's Cello Sonata, vocal works by Schubert and Kalliwoda, and Beethoven's Piano Quartet Op. 16(a). Song texts and program notes for Kalliwoda's 'Heimatlied', Schubert's 'Romanze' from Die Verschworenen, and Gwyneth Walker's 'The Great Trees'. The program explores nature-inspired themes and domestic dramas in song, spanning from the early 19th century to contemporary works. Song texts and program notes for Kalliwoda's 'Heimatlied', Schubert's 'Romanze' from Die Verschworenen, and Gwyneth Walker's 'The Great Trees'. The program explores nature-inspired themes and domestic dramas in song.

'Clarinet Fiesta' featuring Horovitz's Sonatina for clarinet and piano (1981), Milhaud's Suite Op. 157b, Brahms's Clarinet Trio Op. 114, and several arrangements for clarinet choir including works by J.S. Bach, Paul Simon, and Ramin Djawadi. The program highlights the versatility of the clarinet.

Duplicate of Entry 61, 'Clarinet Fiesta' program featuring works by Horovitz, Milhaud, Brahms, and arrangements for clarinet choir. The notes discuss Horovitz's jazz-influenced Sonatina and Milhaud's diverse instrumental combinations.

Duplicate of Entry 63, featuring Ben Hoadley's nature-inspired wind trio, Bach string quartet arrangements, Schubert's Nocturne, Strauss's Violin Sonata, and Brahms's Piano Trio No. 3. Features Ben Hoadley's 'Four Preludes' for wind trio, two movements by J.S. Bach arranged for string quartet, Schubert's 'Nocturne' Op. 148, Strauss's Violin and Piano Sonata, Khachaturian's Trio for clarinet, violin, and piano, and Brahms's Piano Trio No. 3. Includes notes on Hoadley's inspiration fromGrey Butcherbird calls. Features Handel's Sonata in F major for violin, viola, and piano, and Haydn's Quartet Op. 64 No. 5 'The Lark'. Notes explore the history of Handel's settling in England and Haydn's 'Tost' quartets, written during his final year of employment at the Esterhazy court. Features Handel's Sonata in F major, Haydn's 'Lark' Quartet, Elena Kats-Chernin's 'Three Short Trios' for woodwind, and Dvořák's Quartet Op. 51 'Slavic'. The program highlights a mix of Baroque, Classical, and modern Australian works.

Duplicate of Entry 66, featuring Handel, Haydn, Kats-Chernin, and Dvořák's 'Slavic' quartet. The notes focus on the development of the concerto form and the influence of folk music on 19th-century composers. Mendelssohn's Viola Sonata in C minor, C.P.E. Bach's Six Sonatas for clarinet, bassoon, and piano, Hugo Wolf's 'Six German Songs', and Dvořák's Piano Quintet No. 2 Op. 81. The program showcases early Romantic and late 19th-century chamber music.

Duplicate of Entry 69, featuring Schubert's Octet in F, Boëllmann's Cello Sonata, and Richard Strauss's early Violin Sonata. The program emphasizes Romantic lyricism and technical virtuosity. Schubert's Octet in F (movements), Léon Boëllmann's Cello Sonata Op. 40, and Richard Strauss's Violin Sonata Op. 18. The notes describe Schubert's Octet as a model of Beethoven's Septet and highlight Boëllmann's short but prolific career.

Duplicate of Entry 71, featuring Schumann, Blake, Glazunov, Bridge, Fauré, and Shostakovich. The notes discuss Howard Blake's film music roots and Shostakovich's incidental music for 'The Gadfly'. Robert Schumann's 'Adagio and Allegro' Op. 70, Howard Blake's Trio Op. 25, Glazunov's 'Elegy' Op. 4, Frank Bridge's 'Miniatures', Fauré's 'Dolly Suite', and Shostakovich's 'The Gadfly Suite'. A diverse program of short chamber works.

Beethoven's String Quartet Op. 18 No. 2, Brahms's Viola Sonata Op. 120 No. 2, Mozart's 'Kegelstatt' Trio, and Beethoven's 'Spring' Sonata Op. 24. The program features cornerstone works of the Classical and Romantic chamber repertoire.

Duplicate of Entry 73, featuring Beethoven's Op. 18 No. 2 quartet, Brahms's viola sonata, Mozart's 'Kegelstatt' trio, and Beethoven's 'Spring' sonata. Notes explore Beethoven's early discomfort with the string quartet form. Smetana's Piano Trio Op. 15, clarinet duets by Magnani and Mike Curtis, and Boëllmann's Piano Quartet Op. 10. Smetana's trio is an elegiac work written in memory of his daughter.

J.S. Bach's 'Sheep May Safely Graze', Beethoven's Violin Sonata No. 3 Op. 12, J.H. Roman's 'Drottningholm Music', Gipsy Kings' 'Inspiration', and Mozart's Divertimento K439b. A wide-ranging program from Baroque to contemporary crossover.

Beethoven's Violin Sonata No. 3 Op. 12, solo guitar pieces by Bach, Piazzolla, and Houghton, Richard Strauss's Five Lieder, and Boëllmann's Piano Quartet Op. 10 finale. A program combining German Lieder, guitar miniatures, and French chamber music. Beethoven's Violin Sonata Op. 12 No. 3, guitar solos by Bach and Piazzolla, songs by Richard Strauss and from movies, and chamber works by Boëllmann and Piazzolla. The program blends classical masterpieces with popular and film music. Duplicate of Entry 77, featuring Schubert's Trio No. 1, Saint-Saëns's 'Danse Macabre', and Grieg's Violin Sonata No. 3. Includes detailed storytelling about the 'Devil in music' in Danse Macabre. German song translations for Richard Strauss's 'Heimliche Aufforderung', 'Allerseelen', 'Traum durch die Dämmerung', 'Morgen!', and 'Zueignung'. Schubert's Trio No. 1 Op. 99, Saint-Saëns's 'Danse Macabre', Romantic songs by Brodszky, d'Hardelot, and Gade, and Grieg's Violin Sonata No. 3 Op. 45. Notes describe the light-hearted nature of Schubert's final works.

Duplicate of Entry 82, featuring Fauré's Piano Quartet Op. 15, Grieg's Violin Sonata Op. 13, and Heiden's Trio. Includes detailed notes on Fauré's relationship with the Viardot family. Gabriel Fauré's Piano Quartet No. 1 Op. 15, Grieg's Violin Sonata No. 2 Op. 13, and Bernhard Heiden's Trio. Notes highlight Fauré's mastery of harmony and the optimistic nature of his early masterpiece.

Christmas program featuring Corelli's 'Christmas Concerto', Dvořák's String Quartet Op. 51, Schumann's Violin Sonata No. 2, and choral carols by Schütz, Byrd, and others. Duplicate of Entry 84, featuring Corelli's Christmas Concerto, Dvořák's Quartet Op. 51, Schumann's Violin Sonata No. 2, and various Christmas carols.

Duplicate of Entry 86, including Haydn's String Quartet Op. 77 No. 1, Mozart's Violin Sonata K. 526, Doppler's 'Fantasy on Rigoletto', and Mozart's Divertimento K. 563. Haydn's String Quartet Op. 77 No. 1 and Mozart's Violin Sonata K. 526. The notes describe Haydn's quartet as one of his most elaborate and Mozart's sonata as his most virtuosic for violin and piano.

Duplicate of Entry 88, featuring Glinka's Viola Sonata, Fauré's Trio Op. 120, Howard Blake's Trio, and Bruch's Double Concerto. Duplicate of Entry 90, featuring Grieg's Violin Sonata No. 3, cello pieces by Bruch and Fauré, Brahms's Trio Op. 114, and Gershwin arrangements for piano quartet. Glinka's Viola Sonata, Fauré's Piano Trio Op. 120, Howard Blake's woodwind trio, and Bruch's Double Concerto for Clarinet and Viola. Highlights Glinka as the 'Father of Russian Music'. Grieg's Violin Sonata No. 3 Op. 45, short cello works by Bruch and Saint-Saëns, and Brahms's Clarinet Trio Op. 114. Notes discuss the Norwegian flavor of Grieg's sonata and Brahms's late-life inspiration from the clarinet.

Duplicate of Entry 92, featuring Thuille's Sextet, Dvořák's Piano Quintet Op. 81, and a variety of woodwind and saxophone chamber music. Ludwig Thuille's Sextet Op. 6, Dvořák's Piano Quintet Op. 81, Mel Bonis's woodwind trio, and a selection of jazz and popular arrangements for saxophone quartet.

Duplicate of Entry 94, featuring Respighi's Violin Sonata, guitar ensemble works, and wind quartet music by Iradier and others. Respighi's Violin Sonata in B minor, guitar ensemble arrangements of Bach and Carulli, lute duets, and wind quartet music. Includes announcements for local Mosman and Sydney Guitar School concerts.

Beethoven's Violin Sonata No. 4, Haydn's Keyboard Trio Hob. XV:16, clavichord works by C.P.E. Bach, Prokofiev's Flute Sonata Op. 94, and Gershwin arrangements. Notes compare Beethoven's sonata pairs and highlight C.P.E. Bach's love for the clavichord. Duplicate of Entry 96, featuring Beethoven's Op. 23 sonata, Haydn's trio, clavichord pieces, and Prokofiev's Flute Sonata. Includes extensive notes on Prokofiev's neoclassical style. Vocal works by Schubert ('The Shepherd on the Rock'), Prokofiev's Flute Sonata, Brahms's Clarinet Trio, and viola works by Miriam Hyde and Nino Rota. A program focusing on lyricism across the 19th and 20th centuries.

Brahms's Clarinet Trio Op. 114, Yokoyama's 'L'Epice d'Asie' for woodwind trio, guitar duos, and Manuel de Falla's 'Seven Spanish Popular Songs'. Notes discuss Brahms's inspiration from clarinetist Richard Mühlfeld. Duplicate of Entry 99, featuring Brahms's Clarinet Trio, Yokoyama's 'L'Epice d'Asie', and Falla's 'Seven Spanish Popular Songs'. Includes notes on Falla's fastidious composition style and Paris years. Song texts and translations for Manuel de Falla's 'Seven Popular Spanish Songs' (1913), including 'El Paño Moruno', 'Seguidilla Murciana', 'Asturiana', 'Jota', 'Nana', 'Canción', and 'Polo'.

Duplicate of Entry 102, featuring Frackenpohl, Scarlatti, C.P.E. Bach, J.S. Bach, and Dvořák's Piano Quintet. Includes notes on the rarity of performance for some English seafaring songs. Duplicate of Entry 104, containing the lyrics for Scarlatti's 'Gia il sole dal Gange' and Schumann's 'Die beiden Grenadiere' with English translations. French song texts and translations for 'Une Flûte Invisible' (Saint-Saëns), 'Soir Païen' (Gaubert), and other works by Ravel and Diémer. Program featuring Arthur Frackenpohl's 'Waltz and Rag', a selection of songs by Scarlatti and Schumann, C.P.E. Bach's Trio for Clarinet, Bassoon and Piano, J.S. Bach's Cello Suite No. 1, and Dvořák's Piano Quintet No. 2 Op. 81. Program featuring César Franck's Sonata in A major (viola version), Kristofer Spike's 'Wombat Dance' and 'Cheerful Traveller', Poulenc's Sextet for winds and piano, and four French songs by Saint-Saëns, Gaubert, Ravel, and Diémer. Song lyrics for Alessandro Scarlatti's 'Gia il sole dal Gange' and Robert Schumann's 'Die beiden Grenadiere'. The texts describe nature imagery and the return of French soldiers from Russia.

Duplicate of Entry 108, featuring Fauré's Piano Trio, Mendelssohn's String Quartet No. 1, Grandval's woodwind trio, and Shostakovich's String Quartet No. 6. Includes notes on the 'happy' sounds of Shostakovich's 6th quartet. Program includes Fauré's Piano Trio Op. 120, Mendelssohn's String Quartet No. 1, Clemence Grandval's 'Trio de Salon', and Shostakovich's String Quartet No. 6. Fauré wrote his trio while almost completely deaf at age 78.

Beethoven's Violin Sonata No. 4 Op. 23, Albeniz's 'Asturias', Mahler's Piano Quartet in A minor, and a selection of songs by Schumann, Offenbach, and others. The program also features Tchaikovsky's Piano Concerto No. 1 (slow movement). Duplicate of Entry 110, featuring Schubert, Brahms, and Ravel piano trios, alongside guitar ensemble pieces by Thomas Robinson, Karl Jenkins, and others. Schubert's String Quartet Op. 125 No. 1, Brahms's Piano Trio Op. 101, Ravel's Piano Trio in A minor (first movement), and guitar ensemble works. Notes discuss the rhythmic complexity and sparse elegance of Brahms's late chamber music. Song texts for a selection of vocal works including Gade's 'Jealousy', Lehar's 'You are my Heart's Delight', and Schumann's 'The Two Grenadiers'.

Duplicate of Entry 114, featuring Bach, Arensky, and Christmas choral music. Includes a special birthday performance of Bach's sonata by cellist Rachel Scott. J.S. Bach's Flute Sonata BWV 1034 (arranged for flute and cello), Arensky's Piano Trio No. 1 Op. 32, and a selection of Christmas music by Praetorius, Chesnokov, and Holst. Notes highlight Arensky's trio as being influenced by Mendelssohn.

Chamber Music Showcase featuring works by Bruch, including selections from his 'Eight Pieces for Clarinet, Viola and Piano' Op. 83. Includes information on the Amateur Chamber Music Society's history and mission. Notes on Richard Strauss's Cello Sonata in F major, Op. 6. The work is described as having a unified thematic structure influenced by Beethoven and Schumann, with a finale drawing on Wagner and Mendelssohn.

Duplicate of Entry 118, featuring Brahms's Piano Quintet, ANZAC centenary songs, and Poulenc's Sextet. Includes extensive notes on the development of Brahms's Op. 34 from a string quintet to a piano quintet. Program featuring Brahms's Piano Quintet Op. 34, 'Music of the ANZACs' songs by Wainwright and Hyde, and Poulenc's Sextet for piano and winds. Notes detail Brahms's childhood in Hamburg and the compositional history of the quintet. Song texts and program notes for ANZAC centenary songs, including 'I Wonder...' (Wainwright) and 'Anzac Threnody' (Hyde). The songs reflect on the Gallipoli peninsula and the tragedy of war.

Duplicate of Entry 121, featuring Brahms, Turina, and Gershwin. Includes biographical notes on Brahms's early life in Hamburg and his meeting with the Schumanns. Program featuring Brahms's Piano Quintet Op. 34, Joaquin Turina's Piano Quartet Op. 67, and Gershwin arrangements. Notes detail the evolution of the Brahms quintet from a string quintet and Turina's Spanish nationalist style.

Duplicate of Entry 123, featuring Strauss's lieder, Mozart's quintet, and Franck's sonata. Duplicate of Entry 123, featuring Strauss, Mozart, and Franck. Includes historical context on Franck's sonata as a wedding present for violinist Eugène Ysaÿe. Program includes Richard Strauss's Five Lieder, Mozart's String Quintet No. 3 (arranged for flute quintet), and César Franck's Sonata in A major (arranged for viola). Notes highlight Strauss's deep understanding of the female voice.

Beethoven's Violin Sonata No. 7 Op. 30 No. 2, Dvořák's Piano Quintet Op. 81 (movement), Beethoven's Cello Sonata Op. 5 No. 2, and Louise Farrenc's Trio Op. 45. Notes describe the violent contrast and fury of Beethoven's Op. 30 No. 2. Duplicate of Entry 126, featuring Beethoven, Dvořák, and Farrenc. Includes notes on Farrenc's Trio as her last major chamber work and Beethoven's stay at the Heiligenstadt resort.

Chamber Music Showcase at The Independent Theatre featuring Poulenc's Flute Sonata, Louise Farrenc's Trio in E minor Op. 45, Richard Strauss's Five Songs, and Fauré's Violin Sonata No. 1. Duplicate of Entry 128, featuring Schubert, Poulenc, and Brahms. Includes detailed notes on the 'theatrical style' of Schubert's early sacred works. Schubert's 'Erstes Offertorium' D.136 and 'The Shepherd on the Rock' D.965, Poulenc's Flute Sonata, and Brahms's Piano Quartet No. 1 Op. 25. The program covers works from the beginning and end of Schubert's career.

Duplicate of Entry 131, featuring Rimsky-Korsakov, Martinu, woodwind quartet music, and Beethoven. Includes biographical notes on Martinu's early life in a church belfry. Program featuring Rimsky-Korsakov's 'Song of the Indian Guest', Martinu's Sonata for flute, violin, and piano, woodwind quartet pieces by Bozza and Albéniz, and Beethoven's Cello Sonata No. 3 Op. 69. Notes describe Martinu's style as having 'spiky dissonances'.

Duplicate of Entry 131 and 132, despite the label 'Oct 2015'. Features Rimsky-Korsakov, Martinu, woodwind quartet pieces, and Beethoven's Cello Sonata No. 3. Program listing Mendelssohn's Piano Trio in D minor and Viola Sonata, Mozart's String Quartets in D minor and C major, and a Quantz Trio Sonata.

Duplicate of Entry 135, featuring Grieg, Kreutzer, Brahms, and Gershwin. Includes notes on the inspiration for Brahms's late chamber works. Grieg's Violin Sonata No. 3 Op. 45, Conradin Kreutzer's Trio Op. 43, Brahms's Clarinet Trio Op. 114, and Gershwin arrangements. Notes compare Grieg's cosmopolitan style in Op. 45 with his earlier Norwegian-inspired works.
Program featuring Brahms's 'Six Songs', Spohr's 'Four German Songs', French songs by Saint-Saëns, Gaubert, and Ravel, and a selection of Christmas carols by Pitoni, Talley, and others.

Chamber Music for Pleasure program featuring John Carmichael's 'Thredbo Suite', Elena Kats-Chernin's 'Dance of the Paper Umbrellas', and pieces by Albéniz, Bizet, and Joplin. Also includes songs by Debussy and Robert Schumann's Violin Sonata No. 2. Clarinet-focused program featuring Grundman's 'Caprice', Paul Carr's 'Five English Postcards', Haydn's Keyboard Trio Hob. XV:18, and pieces by Cui, Debussy, and Sullivan. Notes highlight the cheerful playfulness of Haydn's later trios. Duplicate of Entry 138, featuring Grundman, Carr, Haydn, Cui, Debussy, and Sullivan. Includes notes on Paul Carr's Sketches illustrating traditional English life.

Duplicate of Entry 141, featuring Shostakovich's waltzes and Duparc's songs. Includes brief biographical notes on both composers. Duplicate of Entry 143, containing the French texts and English translations for Duparc's four songs. Program featuring Shostakovich's 'Four Waltzes' for flute, clarinet, and piano, and four songs by Henri Duparc. Notes describe the contrast between Shostakovich's light, humorous waltzes and the dark political background of the 1950s. Song texts and translations for Henri Duparc's 'L'Invitation au Voyage', 'Lamento', 'Elégie', and 'Phidylé'. The poems explore themes of romantic longing and nature.

Duplicate of Entry 145, featuring Ibert, Weber, and Haydn. Includes notes on Weber's well-written but often overlooked chamber music for wind instruments. Program featuring Jacques Ibert's 'Trois Pièces Brèves', Carl Maria von Weber's Trio in G minor Op. 63, and Haydn's String Quartet Op. 64 No. 5 'The Lark'. Notes discuss Ibert's diverse and light-hearted style.

Beethoven's Violin Sonata No. 6 Op. 30 No. 1, Alexander Zemlinsky's Trio in D minor Op. 3, and Schubert's 'Trout' Quintet D. 667. Notes describe Beethoven's sonata as a work of elegance and tenderness. Duplicate of Entry 147, featuring Beethoven, Zemlinsky, and Schubert. Includes detailed historical context for the 'Trout' Quintet.

Chamber Music for Pleasure at The Independent Theatre featuring works by Grundman, Paul Carr, Kristofer Spike, Louis Spohr, and Arthur Frackenpohl. The program also includes César Franck's Violin Sonata in A major. Duplicate of Entry 150 and 151, listing pieces by Grundman, Carr, Spike, Spohr, Frackenpohl, and Franck. Duplicate of Entry 150, showcasing a diverse program of 19th and 20th-century chamber works for woodwinds, strings, and piano. Program featuring Peter Müller's Wind Quintet No. 2, Louise Farrenc's Trio in E minor Op. 45, Debussy's Piano Trio in G major, and Beethoven's 'Spring' Sonata Op. 24. Includes biographical notes on Peter Müller.

Beethoven's Violin Sonata No. 1 Op. 12 No. 1, Brahms's 'Hungarian Dance Suite No. 1' (arr. Webster), and Beethoven's Violin Sonata No. 3 Op. 12 No. 3. Notes discuss Beethoven's family background and early career in Bonn. Duplicate of Entry 153, featuring Beethoven's early violin sonatas and Brahms's Hungarian dances. Includes extensive notes on the influence of Beethoven's grandfather and father on his musical development.

Chamber Music Showcase featuring Beethoven's 'Gassenhauer' Trio Op. 11, Conradin Kreutzer's Trio Op. 43, Lennox Berkeley's Concertino Op. 49, Joe Wolfe's Quartet, and Brahms's Viola Sonata Op. 120. Duplicate of Entry 155, featuring Brahms, Berkeley, Schubert, Kreutzer, and Mozart. Duplicate of Entry 157 and 158, listing the program for the Chamber Music Showcase. Duplicate of Entry 157, providing a concise listing of the showcase program at The Independent Theatre. Program featuring Brahms's Viola Sonata No. 1 Op. 120, Lennox Berkeley's Concertino Op. 49, Schubert's 'Lebensstürme' Op. 144, Conradin Kreutzer's Trio Op. 43, and Mozart's Symphony No. 40 (arr. Hummel).

Duplicate of Entry 160, featuring Stamitz, Beethoven, and Bridge. Includes detailed analysis of the movements in the Beethoven Duet in C major. Program featuring Karl Stamitz's Quartet Op. 8 No. 4, Beethoven's Duet in C major (arranged for violin and viola), and Frank Bridge's Cello Sonata. Notes explain that the Beethoven duet was likely composed by someone else but remains in his catalogue.

Duplicate of Entry 162, featuring Sibelius, Fauré, and various Christmas carols. Program includes Jean Sibelius's Sonatine Op. 80, Fauré's Violin Sonata No. 1 Op. 13, and a selection of Christmas music by Britten, Praetorius, and Buxtehude. Notes highlight Sibelius's late start with formal violin intuition.

Chamber Music Showcase featuring a range of music and highlighting the benefits of ACMS membership, including playing days and an annual music camp. Duplicate of Entry 164, featuring Haydn, Spike, Carmichael, Shostakovich, and Debussy. Includes background on John Carmichael's career as a pianist and musical therapist. Haydn's String Quartet Op. 20 No. 2, Kristofer Spike's 'Arabesque', John Carmichael's 'Thredbo Suite', Shostakovich's 'Two Pieces' for string quartet, and Debussy's 'Petite Suite'. Notes describe the 'Thredbo Suite' as a lyrical Australian work.

Concert poster for a program featuring works by Mendelssohn, Beethoven, Liszt, and Martinu at the Kirribilli Neighbourhood Centre. Duplicate of Entry 168, listing the composers for the April 2013 concert at Kirribilli. Duplicate of Entry 170, with detailed notes on the nickname 'A Sigh' for Liszt's third concert study and its use in popular film. Mendelssohn's Violin Sonata in F major (1838), Beethoven's Adagio Grazioso from Sonata No. 16, Martinu's Trio H. 300, and Liszt's 'Un Sospiro'. Notes focus on Mendelssohn's early childhood as a prodigy and his friendship with Schumann. Program at Waterbrook featuring Liszt's 'Concert Étude No. 3', Mendelssohn's Violin Sonata in F major, Mozart's Rondo in A minor, Rachmaninov's Élégie, Ravel's 'Noble and Sentimental Waltzes', and Franck's Sonata in A major (arranged for cello).

Program featuring Beethoven's Cello Sonata Op. 5 No. 2, Brahms's Piano Trio Op. 87, Chopin's Ballade No. 3, Schubert's String Quartet No. 13 'Rosamunde', and Piazzolla's 'Bordel 1900'. Program from May 2010 (not 2013 as labeled) featuring Haydn's Trio No. 23, Martinu's Trio H. 300 and 'Promenades', Copland's 'Quiet City', and Dinicu's 'Hora Staccato'.

Chamber Music Showcase featuring Brahms's Clarinet Sonata No. 1, Schubert's 'Rosamunde' Quartet, and works by Mozart. Duplicate of Entry 174, featuring Mozart, Dvořák, and Schumann. Includes analysis of Mozart's creative use of sonata form in the 'Hunt' quartet. Mozart's 'Hunt' Quartet K. 458, Dvořák's 'American' Quartet Op. 96, and Schumann's Violin Sonata No. 2 Op. 121. Notes explore the hunting horn theme in Mozart's quartet and Dvořák's stay in Spillville, Iowa.

Program at The Independent Theatre featuring Schubert's Piano Trio No. 2 Op. 100, Schumann's 'Fantasy Pieces' Op. 73, and Shostakovich's Piano Quintet Op. 57. Notes highlight the ground-breaking structure of Schubert's late trios.

'Three Pieces inspired by Water' featuring Debussy's 'Reflections in the Water', Hyde's 'Valley of Rocks', and Liszt's 'Fountains at the Villa d'Este'. Also includes Schumann's 'Fantasy Pieces' Op. 73 and Schubert's Piano Trio No. 2 Op. 100. Duplicate of Entry 178, featuring water-inspired piano works by Debussy, Hyde, and Liszt, alongside major chamber works by Schumann and Schubert.

Duplicate of Entry 180, featuring Bach, Harris, Elgar, Debussy, Takemitsu, and Telemann. Includes notes on Takemitsu's use of the 'S.E.A.' (Eb-E-A) theme. J.S. Bach's Trio Sonata from 'The Musical Offering', Ross Harris's 'For Eve's Birthday', Elgar's Romance Op. 62, Debussy's 'Clair de Lune', Takemitsu's 'Between Tides', and Telemann's 'Tafelmusik'. The program explores themes of water and celebratory music.

Duplicate of Entry 182, featuring Schubert, Grieg, and Franck. Includes detailed movement analysis for each work. Schubert's String Trio D. 471, Grieg's Cello Sonata Op. 36, Schubert's Grand Sonata for piano four hands D. 617, and César Franck's Violin Sonata in A major. Notes discuss Grieg's discovery of his unique Norwegian musical voice.

Duplicate of Entry 184, featuring Schubert, alongside Mozart's Duo in G major K. 423, Piazzolla, and Kristofer Spike's 'Summer Waltz'. Schubert program including 'The Shepherd on the Rock' D. 965 and the String Quartet in G minor D. 173. Includes full German text and English translation for 'The Shepherd on the Rock'.

Duplicate of Entry 186, featuring clarinet duos and Schumann's Piano Quintet. Includes historical context on the political turmoil in Europe during Schumann's youth. Program featuring clarinet duos by Richard Rodney Bennett, Alan Frank, and Ernesto Cavallini, and Robert Schumann's Piano Quintet Op. 44. Notes explore Schumann's early life and self-taught composition style.

Chamber Music Showcase featuring Spike, Kats-Chernin, Schubert, and Mozart. Notes focus on Jack Mundey's role in saving Sydney's historic buildings and the classical model of Schubert's early quartet. Duplicate of Entry 190, featuring Spike, Kats-Chernin, Schubert, Mozart, Webster's 'Carmen' Rhapsody, and Khachaturian's Trio. Fauré's 'Dolly Suite' (arr. Webster), Martinu's Sextet H. 174, and Dvořák's 'Dumky' Trio Op. 90. Notes describe the personal origins of the 'Dolly Suite' for the daughter of Emma Bardac. Program featuring Kristofer Spike's 'Thank You Jack Mundey', Elena Kats-Chernin's 'Russian Rag', Schubert's String Quartet in G minor D. 173, Mozart's Duo in G major K. 423, and Khachaturian's Trio (1932).

Beethoven's String Quartet Op. 18 No. 2, Beethoven's 'Spring' Sonata Op. 24, and Shostakovich's Piano Quintet Op. 57. Notes contrast the optimism of Beethoven's 'Spring' sonata with the subsequent decline in his health. Concert announcement for 'Cheerful Beethoven and Prize-Winning Shostakovich', featuring string quartet, violin sonata, and piano quintet works. Duplicate of Entry 192, featuring Beethoven and Shostakovich. Includes biographical notes on Shostakovich's difficult relationship with the Soviet government. Duplicate of Entry 194, advertising the June 2012 concert at the Kirribilli Neighbourhood Centre.

Duplicate of Entry 196, featuring Farkas, Gounod, Quilter, Debussy, and Ravel. Includes French text and English translation for Gounod's 'Le Soir'. Program featuring Ferenc Farkas's 'Early Hungarian Dances', Gounod's 'Le Soir', folk song arrangements by Quilter and Britten, Debussy songs, and Ravel's 'Le Tombeau de Couperin'. Notes define 'tombeau' as a musical memorial. Song texts and translations for Roger Quilter's folk song arrangements, Benjamin Britten's 'The Salley Gardens', and songs by Claude Debussy.

Features Mozart's unfinished Violin Sonata K. 372, Chopin's Nocturne Op. 9 No. 2, Haydn's Trio in Eb major, and Brahms's Violin Sonata No. 3. The program also includes Piazzolla's 'Café 1930' and a spiritual work by Estonian composer René Eespere. Program featuring Haydn's Trio in Eb major Hob. XV:10, Schubert's Trio in B flat Op. 99, Brahms's Violin Sonata No. 3 Op. 108, and Piazzolla's 'Café 1930'.
Program includes movements from Debussy's Piano Trio in G major, music for four clarinets by Debussy and Paul Carr, Brahms's Clarinet Sonata Op. 120 No. 1, and Martinu's Trio H. 254.

A program devoted to the works of Franz Schubert, featuring his Impromptu in G flat Op. 90 No. 3, a selection of five Lieder including 'Meeres Stille' and 'Der Musensohn', and his Piano Trio No. 2 in E flat major Op. 100.

Features Fauré's Piano Quartet Op. 15, Peter Müller's Wind Quintet No. 1, Mendelssohn's Piano Trio No. 1, Beethoven's Piano Trio movement WoO-39, and Haydn's 'Gypsy' Trio. Includes biographical notes on Peter Müller.

A varied program featuring Genin's Grand Duo Concertant, Schubert's 'Notturno' D. 897, and Mendelssohn's vocal duets. The concert concludes with Frank Martin's 'Trois Chants de Noël' and arrangements of works by Ronald Binge and J.S. Bach.
